description: Security baseline tailored for K-12 school districts. Adjusts thresholds for education environments with student accounts, shared devices, and limited IT staff. Prioritizes FERPA/COPPA compliance and student data protection.

notes.impossibleTravel: Reduced — students travel between campus buildings and home frequently
notes.concurrentSessions: Reduced — shared devices and Chromebook carts cause concurrent sessions
notes.afterHoursLogin: Reduced — students and teachers access systems at irregular hours
notes.newDevice: Reduced — device rotation is common in shared device environments
notes.driveExternalSharing: Increased — student data shared externally is a FERPA risk
notes.bulkFileDownload: Increased — mass download of student records is high-risk
notes.highRiskOAuthApp: Increased — unvetted apps accessing student data violates COPPA
notes.emailForwardingRule: Increased — auto-forwarding can exfiltrate student PII
notes.adminPrivilegeEscalation: Increased — admin accounts in K-12 have access to student records
notes.domainWideDelegation: Increased — delegation grants full student data access

notes.AUTH-001: MFA enforcement is critical for protecting student data access
notes.OAUTH-001: Third-party app access to student data must be tightly controlled (COPPA)
notes.DRIVE-001: External sharing of Drive files can expose student records (FERPA)

notes.afterHours: Extended hours — teachers and students commonly access systems until late evening
